purpose: npm package manifest and the operational entrypoint definition for Synapse. Declares `electron/main.js` as the application entry, defines five scripts (build/start/dev/dist/release plus postinstall), configures electron-builder for macOS DMG+ZIP packaging with GitHub publishing, and pins runtime/dev dependencies including Electron 41, React 19, Monaco Editor, node-pty, xterm, and electron-updater. The build.files array determines what gets bundled into the .dmg, and the postinstall step rebuilds node-pty against the current Electron ABI.
gotchas: `npm start` is `vite build && unset ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE && electron .` — it builds for production each time, so iterative UI dev should use `npm run dev` instead. The `unset ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE` exists because some shell environments set this var, which would force Electron to run as plain Node and break the GUI.
`postinstall` runs `electron-rebuild -f -w node-pty` — this REQUIRES Xcode Command Line Tools on macOS; without them install fails silently with native compile errors.
Strange dependency: `"nvm": "^0.0.4"` — this is the npm package named 'nvm', NOT the popular shell version manager. It's almost certainly a stale/unused dependency; verify before removing.

build.files INCLUDES `dashboards/**/*` and extraResources copies `dashboards` into the packaged app — packaged Synapse ships with whatever dashboards exist at build time. For clean releases, clear dashboards/ before `npm run dist`.
Targets only `--mac dmg zip` — there is no Windows/Linux build config; `npm run dist` on those platforms will fail or produce an unsigned/unsupported artifact.
Electron-builder publish target points at `DeanCruz/Synapse` GitHub — releases go to that specific repo; forks will need to override.
React 19 is bleeding-edge; some libraries (especially Monaco wrappers) may not have caught up yet.
patterns: Pattern: build chain — `vite build` → produces dist/ → Electron loads dist/index.html via app:// protocol (per electron/main.js).
Pattern: dev chain — `concurrently -k "vite" "electron ."` runs Vite dev server (port 5174 per vite.config.js) AND Electron in parallel; -k kills both when one exits.
Pattern: native module rebuild — `electron-rebuild` pins node-pty's binary ABI to the installed Electron version every time deps change.
Pattern: extraResources + files separation — files are bundled into the asar archive, extraResources are copied alongside (so dashboards/ stays writable on disk after install).
conventions: Versions use caret `^` ranges (allow minor and patch updates).
No `engines` field — Node version is stated only in README.md (18+).
No `type: module` — project mixes CommonJS (electron services) and ESM (vite/react via .jsx).
